is the second-tier [association football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") league in [Namibia](/wiki/Namibia "Namibia"). It operates under the auspices of the [Namibia Football Association](/wiki/Namibia_Football_Association "Namibia Football Association").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [First Division (Namibia)](/w/index.php?title=First_Division_(Namibia)&redirect=no "First Division (Namibia)"))

Football league

Namibia First Division

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Founded | 2006 |
| Country | [Namibia](/wiki/Namibia "Namibia") |
| [Confederation](/wiki/Geography_of_association_football "Geography of association football") | [CAF](/wiki/Confederation_of_African_Football "Confederation of African Football") |
| Number of clubs | 26–39 |
| Level on pyramid | 2 |
| [Promotion](/wiki/Promotion_and_relegation "Promotion and relegation") to | [Namibia Premiership](/wiki/Namibia_Premiership "Namibia Premiership") |
| [Relegation](/wiki/Promotion_and_relegation "Promotion and relegation") to | [Regional Second Division](/wiki/Regional_Second_Division_(Namibia) "Regional Second Division (Namibia)") |

The **First Division** is the second-tier [association football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") league in [Namibia](/wiki/Namibia "Namibia"). It operates under the auspices of the [Namibia Football Association](/wiki/Namibia_Football_Association "Namibia Football Association").

## Organization

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Namibia_First_Division&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Organization")]

The First Division is divided into the Northern Stream (NSFD) and the Southern Stream (SSFD), each consisting of 13 teams from their respective regions.[[1]](#cite_note-Northern_Stream-1)[[2]](#cite_note-Southern_Stream-2) For some seasons the Northern Stream has been divided into separate North East and North West streams.[[3]](#cite_note-Divert_sponsorship-3)

The Northern Stream includes clubs from the [Zambezi](/wiki/Zambezi_Region "Zambezi Region"), [Kavango East](/wiki/Kavango_East_Region "Kavango East Region"), [Kavango West](/wiki/Kavango_West_Region "Kavango West Region"), [Oshana](/wiki/Oshana "Oshana"), [Omusati](/wiki/Omusati "Omusati"), [Kunene](/wiki/Kunene_Region "Kunene Region"), [Otjozondjupa](/wiki/Otjozondjupa "Otjozondjupa"), [Ohangwena](/wiki/Ohangwena "Ohangwena"), and [Oshikoto](/wiki/Oshikoto "Oshikoto") regions.[[1]](#cite_note-Northern_Stream-1)

The Southern Stream includes clubs located in [Khomas](/wiki/Khomas "Khomas"), [Omaheke](/wiki/Omaheke "Omaheke"), [Erongo](/wiki/Erongo "Erongo"), [Hardap](/wiki/Hardap "Hardap"), and [Karas](/wiki/%C7%81Karas_Region "ǁKaras Region") regions.[[2]](#cite_note-Southern_Stream-2)

## Promotion and relegation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Namibia_First_Division&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Promotion and relegation")]

The winners of both the Northern and Southern Streams are promoted to the [Namibia Premier Football League](/wiki/Namibia_Premier_Football_League "Namibia Premier Football League"). The three lowest placed teams from each stream are relegated to the Regional Second Division.[[1]](#cite_note-Northern_Stream-1)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Namibia_First_Division&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: History")]

The league began operating under the now-defunct [Namibia Premier League](/wiki/Namibia_Premier_League "Namibia Premier League") for the 2006/07 season.[[3]](#cite_note-Divert_sponsorship-3) In 2021 the league returned from a hiatus of over three years caused by troubles at the NFA and the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic"). For this special transitional tournament, the league was divided into four streams: Southern, Coastal, Inland, and Central.[[4]](#cite_note-Football_returns-4)[[5]](#cite_note-Resurrected-5)
